hypothesis: A rewritten one-line description with a third 'or proposes a direction...' trigger passes all pre-commit hooks with no other file touched
experiment: Replaced skills/evidence-over-deference/SKILL.md:3 with a 3-trigger one-line 'Use when ...' description; ran `python3 -m pre_commit run --all-files`
observed: Exit 0; all 17 hooks Passed; no sync gate fired despite AGENTS.md:88, README.md:34, cursor:26, copilot:100-104 still carrying the old paraphrase
design_consequence: Machine forces nothing beyond SKILL.md; the 4 prose paraphrases MUST be hand-synced in the same commit or they drift silently

hypothesis: A YAML folded/multiline description fails check-skill-frontmatter
experiment: Replaced description with 'description: >-' + 2 continuation lines; ran scripts/checks/check-skill-frontmatter.sh
observed: Exit 1: "FAIL skills/evidence-over-deference/SKILL.md: 'description:' must start with 'Use when '"
design_consequence: The widened description must remain ONE physical line starting 'description: Use when '; no length cap (MD013 disabled)

hypothesis: Commit-msg gates pass with conventional scoped type + Signed-off-by trailer, fail without
experiment: Ran check-commit-trailer.sh and the conventional-pre-commit hook (--hook-stage commit-msg) against msg-good.txt ('feat(evidence-over-deference): ...' + Signed-off-by) and msg-bad.txt (bare 'update skill', no trailer)
observed: good: trailer exit 0, conventional exit 0; bad: trailer exit 1, conventional exit 1
design_consequence: Final commit uses 'feat(evidence-over-deference): ...' + Signed-off-by/Co-Authored-By trailers; matches the in-place-sharpening naming precedent (508fd90)
